2605.02572.title: On Training Large Language Models for Long-Horizon Tasks: An Empirical Study of Horizon Length
2605.02572.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) have shown promise as interactive agents that solve tasks through extended sequences of environment interactions. While prior work has primarily focused on system-level optimizations or algorithmic improvements, the role of task horizon length in shaping training dynamics remains poorly understood. In this work, we present a systematic empirical study that examines horizon length through controlled task constructions. Specifically, we construct controlled tasks in which agents face identical decision rules and reasoning structures, but differ only in the length of action sequences required for successful completion. Our results reveal that increasing horizon length alone constitutes a training bottleneck, inducing severe training instability driven by exploration difficulties and credit assignment challenges. We demonstrate that horizon reduction is a key principle to address this limitation, stabilizing training and achieving better performance in long-horizon tasks. Moreover, we find that horizon reduction is related to stronger generalization across horizon lengths: models trained under reduced horizons generalize more effectively to longer-horizon variants at inference time, a phenomenon we refer to as horizon generalization.
2605.02572.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）已顯示出作為互動代理的潛力，能通過延長的環境互動序列來解決任務。雖然先前的研究主要集中在系統層級的優化或算法改進上，但任務視野長度在塑造訓練動態中的角色仍然不甚了解。在這項工作中，我們提出了一項系統性的實證研究，通過控制任務構造來檢查視野長度。具體而言，我們構建了控制任務，其中代理面臨相同的決策規則和推理結構，但在成功完成所需的行動序列長度上有所不同。我們的結果顯示，僅僅增加視野長度就構成了一個訓練瓶頸，導致由於探索困難和信用分配挑戰引起的嚴重訓練不穩定性。我們證明，縮短視野是一個關鍵原則，可以解決這一限制，穩定訓練並在長視野任務中實現更好的性能。此外，我們發現縮短視野與在不同視野長度之間更強的泛化能力有關：在縮短視野下訓練的模型在推理時能更有效地泛化到長視野變體，這一現象我們稱之為視野泛化。
2605.02572.author: Sunghwan Kim et.al.
2605.02572.authors: Sunghwan Kim, Junhee Cho, Beong-woo Kwak, Taeyoon Kwon, Liang Wang, Nan Yang, Xingxing Zhang, Furu Wei, Jinyoung Yeo
